name: A Charlie Brown Christmas
description: The 1965 Peanuts classic — Snoopy, Linus, Vince Guaraldi's jazz, and that sad little tree.

questions.q: In what year did 'A Charlie Brown Christmas' first air?
On which network did 'A Charlie Brown Christmas' originally air?

source.notes: Aired on CBS until 2000; later moved to ABC, then Apple TV+ and PBS.

questions.q: Who composed the iconic jazz score for 'A Charlie Brown Christmas'?

source.notes: The trio's 'Linus and Lucy' became the de facto Peanuts theme.

questions.q: What is the running time of the special?
Who originally sponsored the special's first airing?

source.notes: Coca-Cola commissioned the special; references to Coke have been edited out of later airings.

questions.q: Who created the 'Peanuts' comic strip on which the special is based?

source.notes: Schulz wrote the Peanuts strip from 1950 until his death in 2000.

questions.q: Who was the lead animator/director of the special?

source.notes: Melendez also voiced Snoopy and Woodstock.

questions.q: Who voiced Snoopy (and Woodstock) by speeding up vocal recordings of his own voice?
What kind of tree does Charlie Brown bring to the school play?

source.notes: Charlie Brown picks the only real tree among aluminum trees — a swipe at commercialized Christmas.

questions.q: Which character delivers the speech recounting the Nativity story from the Gospel of Luke?

source.notes: Linus drops his blanket during 'Fear not' — the only time in the Peanuts canon.

questions.q: What is Lucy van Pelt running her famous 'sidewalk' service for?

source.notes: 'The doctor is in.' Five-cent psychiatric advice.

questions.q: What musical instrument does Schroeder play in the Peanuts world?
What does Charlie Brown become director of in the special?

source.notes: He's chosen because nobody else wants the job.

questions.q: What does Charlie Brown's sister Sally famously want for Christmas in the special?

source.notes: She dictates a letter to Santa: 'You can just send the money. How about tens and twenties?'

questions.q: Where is Snoopy decorating during the dog-house decorating contest?
What is the title of the show's most famous instrumental piece, also used as the Peanuts theme?

source.notes: Often confused with the actual title but used in countless Peanuts specials.

questions.q: What did the network originally fear about the special's content?
choices: Too jazzy / not enough laugh track

source.notes: CBS executives worried about the jazz score, lack of laugh track, slow pace, and child voices — all of which became its signature elements.

questions.q: How was the special's child cast unconventionally chosen?
choices: Real children, not professional adult voice actors
Random fans of the comic strip

source.notes: A Peanuts trademark from this special onward.

questions.q: Who voiced Charlie Brown in the original 1965 special?

source.notes: Robbins voiced Charlie Brown from 1963 to 1969.

questions.q: Which streaming service exclusively held the special starting in 2020 (with later PBS deal)?

source.notes: Apple TV+ acquired the Peanuts library in 2020; the Christmas, Thanksgiving, and Halloween specials still air on PBS for free.
